_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
source_files: Nerve Repair Surgery, Exploration and Ulnar nerve Repair_Medco.pdf
Nerve Repair Surgery,Exploration and Ulnar nerve Repair_PPD.CPD.pdf
clinical_indications: Injuries, Fractures, Ulnar Pediatric Nerve Tumors, Congenital abnormalities, Functional loss.
Ulnar nerve subluxation: Symptomatic cases needs treatment similar to that of cubital tunnel syndrome.
Ulnar Nerve Compression: The ulnar nerve can be subject to compression at several sites along its course.
Complete high ulnar nerve injury usually causes severe sequelae, such as residual sensation, loss of movement, and “claw hand” deformity, and affects fine manipulation of the hand.
Direct trauma to the ulnar nerve can be watched if the nerve is in continuity or repaired or grafted if the nerve has been severed or ruptured.

Radiological investigations confirming the need of surgery; +/- NCV report

ppd_preauth.q: Clinical notes with history, signs, symptoms, evaluation findings, indication for procedure, planned line of management and advice for admission submitted?
MRI / EMG/ NCV of the affected part labelled with patient ID, date and side (Left/ Right) - confirming the diagnosis submitted?
X-ray of affected part labelled with patient ID, date and side (Left/ Right) submitted?
Radiological investigations confirming the need of surgery; +/- NCV report submitted?

cpd_claim.q: Are Detailed Indoor case papers (ICPs) submitted?
Are the Post procedure clinical photographs submitted?
Are the detailed Procedure / Operative notes submitted?
Is there a Detailed Discharge Summary submitted?

common_queries: Clinical notes with history, signs, symptoms, evaluation findings, indication for procedure, planned line of management and advice for admission are missing or incomplete.
Radiological investigations confirming the need of surgery or NCV report is missing or incomplete.
Clinical photograph of affected part is missing.
The clinical notes and imaging reports submitted are not indicative of surgery.
Detailed Indoor case papers (ICPs) are missing or incomplete.
Detailed procedure / Operative Notes are missing or incomplete.
Discharge summary with follow-up advice at the time of discharge is missing or incomplete.

clinical_key_pointers: The ulnar nerve in particular is a mixed motor and sensory nerve, with a long distance to travel to its end motor targets in the hand, because of that the nerve lesions often have a poor prognosis.
Ulnar nerve conditions in children can be largely grouped into compression (direct or due to mass effect) and trauma (fracture or directly to the nerve). Primary nerve tumors are uncommon.
Complete high ulnar nerve injury usually causes severe sequelae, such as residual sensation, loss of movement, and “claw hand” deformity, and affects fine manipulation of the hand.
The most common causes of trauma to the ulnar nerve in children involve fractures around the elbow and their treatment, namely, supracondylar humerus and medial epicondylar fractures.
The ulnar nerve can also be subject to compression syndromes in children, primarily at the elbow.
Direct trauma to the ulnar nerve can be watched if the nerve is in continuity or repaired or grafted if the nerve has been severed or ruptured.
Diagnosis: Physical examination and sensory functions/signs. Nerve Conduction Studies.
surgical_options: Primary repair: Viable nerve ends, Accurate fascicular alignment, few stitches, minimal tension etc.
Tendon transfer, functional free muscle transfers.
Ulnar Nerve transfers (Distal nerve transfer from the median nerve).
